en: Yes, I can understand you! I am U-Pal, UWTSD's student assistant chatbot. I can help you with: admissions and applications, course information, tuition fees and student finance, accommodation, campus locations, IT support (Moodle, portal, Wi-Fi), library services, student wellbeing, careers, and more. Just ask me a question!

I'm U-Pal, here to help UWTSD students and applicants. You can ask me about admissions, courses, fees, accommodation, IT support, campus locations, the library, wellbeing services, the students' union, graduation, and much more. What would you like to know?
Hi there! I'm working and ready to help. I'm U-Pal — a bilingual chatbot for UWTSD. Ask me anything about studying at UWTSD: applications, courses, fees, accommodation, IT, wellbeing, and more.

Take care! If you need anything else, U-Pal is always here to help.

en: To apply to UWTSD, you can apply directly through uwtsd.ac.uk/study or through UCAS (ucas.com) for undergraduate courses. International students can also apply directly. Choose your course, check entry requirements, and submit your application. The admissions team can help on 01267 676767 or admissions@uwtsd.ac.uk.

Applying to UWTSD is straightforward. For most undergraduate courses, apply via UCAS (ucas.com). For postgraduate or direct entry, go to uwtsd.ac.uk/study. You'll need to choose your campus preference: Swansea, Carmarthen, or Lampeter. Need help choosing a course? Just ask!

Mae gwneud cais i PCYDDS yn syml. Ar gyfer y rhan fwyaf o gyrsiau israddedig, gwnewch gais trwy UCAS (ucas.com). Ar gyfer mynediad ôl-raddedig neu uniongyrchol, ewch i pcydds.ac.uk/study.

en: Entry requirements vary by course at UWTSD. Most undergraduate courses require 80-120 UCAS points (roughly 2-3 A-levels at grade C or above). UWTSD also considers mature students, Access to HE qualifications, and BTECs. Check the specific course page at uwtsd.ac.uk for exact requirements.

Requirements differ by course. UWTSD welcomes students with A-levels, BTECs, Access to HE, Scottish Highers, and Welsh Baccalaureate. Foundation year options are also available if you don't quite meet the standard entry requirements. Check uwtsd.ac.uk/study for your specific course.

Mae gofynion yn amrywio yn ôl cwrs. Mae PCYDDS yn croesawu myfyrwyr â lefelau A, BTECs, Mynediad i AU, Uwch Raddau Albanaidd, a Bagloriaeth Cymru. Mae opsiynau blwyddyn sylfaen hefyd ar gael.

en: The main UCAS deadline for most undergraduate courses is usually late January for September entry. UWTSD also accepts applications through UCAS Extra and Clearing after this date. Postgraduate and part-time courses often have rolling admissions. Check uwtsd.ac.uk/study or call 01267 676767 for the latest deadlines.

Key deadlines: UCAS main deadline is usually 29 January for September entry. UWTSD participates in Clearing from mid-August for students whose results differ from expectations. Postgraduate courses often have flexible deadlines. Email admissions@uwtsd.ac.uk for specifics.
cy: Y prif ddyddiad cau UCAS ar gyfer y rhan fwyaf o gyrsiau israddedig fel arfer yw diwedd Ionawr ar gyfer mynediad ym mis Medi. Mae PCYDDS hefyd yn derbyn ceisiadau trwy UCAS Extra a Chlirio ar ôl y dyddiad hwn.

Dyddiad cau allweddol: Y prif ddyddiad cau UCAS fel arfer yw 29 Ionawr ar gyfer mynediad ym mis Medi. Mae PCYDDS yn cymryd rhan mewn Clirio o ganol mis Awst. Cysylltwch â admissions@uwtsd.ac.uk am fanylion penodol.

en: International students are very welcome at UWTSD! Apply directly through uwtsd.ac.uk/international. You'll likely need a Student Visa if from outside the UK. UWTSD's International Office supports you with visa guidance, language requirements, and pastoral care. Contact international@uwtsd.ac.uk.

UWTSD welcomes students from over 50 countries. Apply via uwtsd.ac.uk/international. Key requirements: proof of English proficiency (usually IELTS 6.0+), academic transcripts, valid passport. The International Office guides you through the visa process at international@uwtsd.ac.uk.
cy: Mae myfyrwyr rhyngwladol yn fwy na chroesawus ym PCYDDS! Gwnewch gais yn uniongyrchol trwy pcydds.ac.uk/international. Bydd angen Fisa Myfyriwr arnoch os ydych chi o'r tu allan i'r DU. Cysylltwch â international@uwtsd.ac.uk.
Mae PCYDDS yn croesawu myfyrwyr o dros 50 gwlad. Gofynion allweddol: tystiolaeth o hyfedredd yn y Saesneg (fel arfer IELTS 6.0+), trawsgrifiadau academaidd, pasbort dilys.

en: UWTSD offers Foundation Year programmes for students who don't quite meet standard entry requirements. Great for mature students, those returning to education, or with non-traditional qualifications. Successfully completing the Foundation Year guarantees progression to Year 1 of your chosen degree. Check uwtsd.ac.uk/study for available pathways.

UWTSD's Foundation Year is a great stepping stone into university. It's designed for students who need extra preparation before degree-level study. After passing, you move directly into Year 1. Ask admissions at admissions@uwtsd.ac.uk about foundation year options.

en: UWTSD offers a huge range of courses including Art and Design, Business, Computing, Education, Engineering, Health and Social Care, Humanities, Law, Nursing, Psychology, Sport Science, Social Work, Welsh and many more. Courses are available at undergraduate, postgraduate, and professional levels. Browse the full list at uwtsd.ac.uk/study.

UWTSD has over 100 courses across its campuses. Highlights include Education, Nursing, Art and Design, Business, Welsh medium courses, and Sustainability. Visit uwtsd.ac.uk/study and filter by subject, level, or campus to find what suits you.

Mae gan PCYDDS dros 100 o gyrsiau ar draws ei champysau. Ymwelwch â pcydds.ac.uk/study a hidlwch yn ôl pwnc, lefel, neu gampws.

en: UWTSD offers Computing and IT-related degrees mainly at the Swansea SA1 campus. Courses include BSc Computer Science, BSc Software Engineering, BSc Cybersecurity, and related programmes. These are industry-focused with strong links to employers. Visit uwtsd.ac.uk/study and search 'computing' for the full list.

UWTSD has a strong Computing department at Swansea. Courses cover programming, networking, cybersecurity, software development, and data science. Many include industry placements. Check uwtsd.ac.uk/study for up-to-date course details and UCAS codes.

en: UWTSD has an excellent reputation for Nursing and Health Sciences. Courses include Adult Nursing (BSc), Mental Health Nursing (BSc), and Health and Social Care programmes. Nursing is primarily based at Swansea campus and is NMC-accredited with clinical placements throughout. Check uwtsd.ac.uk/study for current intake dates.

Nursing at UWTSD is highly regarded. BSc Nursing programmes include real clinical placements across NHS Wales. Based mainly at Swansea with some teaching at Carmarthen. Entry typically requires specific science GCSEs. See uwtsd.ac.uk for details.

en: UWTSD is one of Wales' leading teacher training institutions. Courses include BA Education (Primary), PGCE Primary, PGCE Secondary (various subjects), and BA Childhood Studies. The Carmarthen campus is the main hub for Education, accredited by the Education Workforce Council (EWC) of Wales. Visit uwtsd.ac.uk/study.

Teacher training at UWTSD is mainly at Carmarthen campus. You can train to teach in Welsh-medium or English-medium schools. Programmes include the PGCE and undergraduate BA Education. Placement schools are local and community-focused.

en: Tuition fees at UWTSD for home (UK) students are currently £9,000 per year for most full-time undergraduate courses. Welsh students may be eligible for a Tuition Fee Loan from Student Finance Wales covering the full amount, so nothing is paid upfront. Postgraduate fees vary. See the full fee schedule at uwtsd.ac.uk/study/fees-and-finance/tuition-fee-schedule.

For current entry, UWTSD charges £9,000 per year for full-time undergraduate courses. Welsh-domiciled students can apply for a Tuition Fee Loan to cover this in full, repaid through salary once you earn over the threshold. Part-time fees are pro-rata. Full fee breakdown: uwtsd.ac.uk/study/fees-and-finance/tuition-fee-schedule.
cy: Mae ffioedd dysgu ym PCYDDS ar gyfer myfyrwyr cartref (DU) ar hyn o bryd yn £9,000 y flwyddyn ar gyfer y rhan fwyaf o gyrsiau israddedig amser llawn. Efallai y bydd myfyrwyr Cymreig yn gymwys am Fenthyciad Ffi Dysgu gan Gyllid Myfyrwyr Cymru. Gweler yr amserlen ffioedd lawn: uwtsd.ac.uk/study/fees-and-finance/tuition-fee-schedule.

Mae PCYDDS yn codi £9,000 y flwyddyn ar gyfer cyrsiau israddedig amser llawn. Gall myfyrwyr sy'n byw yng Nghymru wneud cais am Fenthyciad Ffi Dysgu i dalu hyn yn llawn. Gweler yr amserlen ffioedd lawn: uwtsd.ac.uk/study/fees-and-finance/tuition-fee-schedule.

en: Student Finance Wales (studentfinancewales.co.uk) provides Tuition Fee Loans and Maintenance Loans for eligible Welsh-domiciled students. You can borrow up to £9,000 for fees and a maintenance loan towards living costs. You only repay once you earn over £25,000. Applications usually open in spring. Call 0300 200 4050.

Welsh students studying at UWTSD can apply to Student Finance Wales for: 1) Tuition Fee Loan covering fees entirely, 2) Maintenance Loan helping with living costs (amount depends on household income). Apply at studentfinancewales.co.uk. It's free to apply and nothing is paid upfront.
cy: Mae Cyllid Myfyrwyr Cymru (cyllidmyfyrwyrcymru.co.uk) yn darparu Benthyciadau Ffi Dysgu a Benthyciadau Cynhaliaeth ar gyfer myfyrwyr cymwys sy'n byw yng Nghymru. Dim ond ar ôl ennill dros £25,000 y byddwch yn ad-dalu. Ffoniwch 0300 200 4050.
Gall myfyrwyr Cymreig sy'n astudio ym PCYDDS wneud cais i Gyllid Myfyrwyr Cymru am: 1) Benthyciad Ffi Dysgu, 2) Benthyciad Cynhaliaeth. Ymgeisiwch ar cyllidmyfyrwyrcymru.co.uk.

Awards vary from a few hundred to several thousand pounds. Visit uwtsd.ac.uk/scholarships or contact finance@uwtsd.ac.uk for the current list.

Funding opportunities at UWTSD include Welsh-medium study bursaries, sports performance scholarships, subject bursaries, and hardship funds. The Students' Union also has emergency funds. Check uwtsd.ac.uk/scholarships and apply early as spaces are limited.

en: The UWTSD student portal is MyTSD, accessible at mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk (direct login: mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk/urd/sits.urd/run/siw_lgn). Log in with your student ID and enrolment password. Through MyTSD you can view timetables, check module results, manage enrolment tasks, access services, and view important notices. Some reports reflect the last known verified state once key processes have been completed. Login issues? Contact IT Services on 01792 481 111 or itservices@uwtsd.ac.uk.

MyTSD (mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk) is your main UWTSD student portal, powered by the SITS student information system. Log in with your student number and enrolment password to access module results, enrolment tasks, timetables, and application information. It also contains tasks you may need to complete at any point during your application or period of study. Forgotten your password? Use the 'Forgot Password' link on the login page or call IT Services on 01792 481 111.
cy: Porth myfyrwyr PCYDDS yw MyTSD, y gellir ei gyrchu ar mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk (mewngofnodiad uniongyrchol: mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk/urd/sits.urd/run/siw_lgn). Defnyddiwch eich rhif myfyriwr a chyfrinair cofrestru. Drwy MyTSD gallwch weld amserlenni, gwirio canlyniadau modiwlau, rheoli tasgau cofrestru, a gweld hysbysiadau. Os oes problemau mewngofnodi, cysylltwch â Gwasanaethau TG ar 01792 481 111.

MyTSD (mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk) yw eich prif borth myfyrwyr PCYDDS, wedi'i bweru gan system wybodaeth myfyrwyr SITS. Mewngofnodwch gyda'ch rhif myfyriwr a'ch cyfrinair i gyrchu canlyniadau modiwlau, tasgau cofrestru, amserlenni, a gwybodaeth am eich cais. Wedi anghofio eich cyfrinair? Defnyddiwch y ddolen 'Anghofio Cyfrinair' ar y dudalen mewngofnodi.

en: UWTSD uses Moodle as its Virtual Learning Environment (VLE) at moodle.uwtsd.ac.uk. Log in with your student username and password. You'll find course materials, lecture notes, assignment briefs, and submission portals there. Having trouble submitting an assignment? Contact your lecturer or course lead directly first, then IT Services at itservices@uwtsd.ac.uk or 01792 481 111 if it's a technical issue.

Moodle (moodle.uwtsd.ac.uk) is UWTSD's online learning platform. All course content, reading lists, recordings, and submission portals are there. Use your student credentials to log in. If you cannot submit an assignment, let your lecturer know straight away — they can grant extensions or accept work by email. For login or access issues, contact IT Services at itservices@uwtsd.ac.uk.

en: UWTSD (University of Wales Trinity Saint David) was formed in 2010 through a merger of Trinity University College Carmarthen and Swansea Metropolitan University, with heritage dating back to 1822 at Lampeter. Known for excellent teaching quality, student satisfaction, and strong links to Welsh culture. Around 10,000 students study here.

UWTSD is a proud Welsh university with 200 years of history. Known for strong teaching, practical courses, and a focus on student wellbeing. It performs well in student satisfaction surveys and is accredited by the Quality Assurance Agency (QAA) with many courses holding professional accreditations.

en: Academic stress is very common and completely understandable. Here's what can help: 1) Talk to your personal tutor, they can advise on extensions or support. 2) Contact Wellbeing at wellbeingsupport@uwtsd.ac.uk. 3) Use the library for study skills help. 4) Break work into smaller chunks. You've got this.

Exam and assignment stress affects most students. UWTSD support includes: your personal tutor who can advise on extensions, Wellbeing at wellbeingsupport@uwtsd.ac.uk for counselling, and study skills workshops through the library. The Students' Union welfare officer can also help with practical issues.

Great question — time management is one of the most valuable skills at university! Here are some proven strategies: 1) Use a planner or calendar (Google Calendar, Outlook) to block study time for each module. 2) Break large assignments into weekly milestones — never leave a dissertation to the last few weeks. 3) Try the Pomodoro technique: 25 minutes focused work, 5-minute break. 4) Prioritise by deadline and difficulty — tackle the hardest task first when your energy is highest. 5) UWTSD's Study Skills team and your personal tutor can help you build a personalised plan — contact studentservices@uwtsd.ac.uk or 01792 481 111.

Dyma rai strategaethau profedig: 1) Defnyddiwch ddyddiadur neu galendr (Google Calendar, Outlook) i neilltuo amser astudio ar gyfer pob modiwl. 2) Rhannwch aseiniadau mawr yn filltirfeini wythnosol. 3) Rhowch gynnig ar dechneg Pomodoro: 25 munud o waith wedi'i ffocysu, 5 munud o seibiant. 4) Blaenoriaethwch yn ôl dyddiad cau ac anhawster. 5) Gall tîm Sgiliau Astudio PCYDDS a'ch tiwtor personol helpu — studentservices@uwtsd.ac.uk neu 01792 481 111.

en: Enrolment at UWTSD is completed through the MyTSD portal at mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk. Log in to find your enrolment tasks under the 'Enrolment Details' section. You'll typically need to upload proof of identity and qualifications, confirm your personal details, and complete any outstanding tasks. The portal also shows your Applicant section, Documents Required, Marks and Exam Board decisions, and Graduation application. Some information reflects the last verified state once key processes are complete.

To enrol, log into MyTSD at mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk/urd/sits.urd/run/siw_lgn. Your portal contains everything you need: enrolment tasks, document uploads, data changes, applicant information, marks and exam board outcomes, and graduation applications. Complete all outstanding tasks as soon as possible — some sections may only update after specific processes have been completed by the university. For help, contact Student Registry at registry@uwtsd.ac.uk.

Bydd angen i chi fel arfer uwchlwytho prawf adnabod a chymwysterau, cadarnhau eich manylion personol, a chwblhau unrhyw dasgau sy'n weddill.

en: UWTSD uses several key digital systems: MyTSD (mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk) is your student information portal for enrolment, results, timetables, and tasks. Moodle (moodle.uwtsd.ac.uk) is the Virtual Learning Environment for course materials, lecture notes, and assignment submissions. Your UWTSD email is accessed via Microsoft Outlook/Office 365. IT help is available at itservices@uwtsd.ac.uk or 01792 481 111.

The main platforms you need as a UWTSD student are: MyTSD (mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk) for your academic record, enrolment, results, and portal tasks — Moodle (moodle.uwtsd.ac.uk) for course content, readings, and submissions — Microsoft 365 for email and Office apps — and the UWTSD website (uwtsd.ac.uk) for general information and services. All use your student credentials to log in.

en: It sounds like you're working on your dissertation — that's a big undertaking and it's completely normal to need some guidance! Your dissertation supervisor is your main point of contact for content and structure. Find their details on Moodle or MyTSD (mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk). If you're worried about a deadline or need an extension, contact your programme administrator or Student Services: studentservices@uwtsd.ac.uk | 01792 481 111.

Dissertation stress is very real — you're not alone in this! Your go-to people are: (1) your dissertation supervisor for academic guidance, (2) your Personal Tutor for pastoral support, and (3) the Wellbeing team (wellbeingsupport@uwtsd.ac.uk) if you're finding it tough. For deadline extensions, speak to your programme administrator as early as possible.

en: UWTSD has a strong support network for students who need academic help. Your first step is your Personal Tutor — they're there to help with any academic concerns and can point you to the right resources. Find their details on Moodle or MyTSD (mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk). For additional learning support (including dyslexia, study skills, and exam preparation), contact ALSS: alss@uwtsd.ac.uk | 01792 481 111.

Struggling a bit? That's OK — reaching out is the right move! Your Personal Tutor and module leaders are your first port of call. UWTSD's Additional Learning Support Service (ALSS) also offers study skills workshops, one-to-one support, and exam accommodations: alss@uwtsd.ac.uk. Student Services at studentservices@uwtsd.ac.uk can also help you find the right support.
cy: Mae PCYDDS yn cynnig rhwydwaith cymorth cryf i fyfyrwyr sydd angen cymorth academaidd. Eich cam cyntaf yw eich Tiwtor Personol — mae ef/hi yno i helpu gydag unrhyw bryderon academaidd. Gallwch ddod o hyd i'w manylion ar Moodle neu MyTSD (mytsd.uwtsd.ac.uk). Am gymorth dysgu ychwanegol (gan gynnwys dyslecsia, sgiliau astudio), cysylltwch ag ALSS: alss@uwtsd.ac.uk | 01792 481 111.

For wellbeing, the Wellbeing Hub on campus is a warm, welcoming drop-in space. For everything else, Student Services reception is open Mon–Fri, 9am–5pm.
